steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim has two parts: Lady Caroline feels bitterness AND she conceals it from her companions. The correct choice must show both her hidden inner state and a charming outer appearance simultaneously.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• ""And Lady Caroline, sitting where [the others] had left her before breakfast on the wall, peeped over when she heard laughter, and saw them standing on the path below, and thought what a mercy it was they were laughing down there and had not come up and done it round her."": Does not show both the inner bitterness and the outward concealment together. ✗
  • ""The idea that [Lady Caroline] would go on giving orders was too absurd. She never gave orders at home. Nobody there dreamed of asking her to do anything. That such a very tiresome activity should be thrust upon her here, simply because she happened to be able to talk Italian, was ridiculous."": Also missing the explicit pairing of hidden disillusionment and charming exterior. ✗
  • ""'What does one do with people like this?' [Lady Caroline] asked herself, her eyes fixed on Mrs. Fisher in what felt to her an indignant stare but appeared to Mrs. Fisher as really charming docility."": Shows a flicker of contrast (indignant vs. charming) but never names the underlying bitterness itself. ✗
  • ""A deep cynicism took hold of the unhappy [Lady Caroline]. Her inside grew hoary with disillusionment, while her gracious and charming outside continued to make the world more beautiful."": Directly names the bitterness (disillusionment) and shows its concealment through the explicit inside-versus-outside contrast. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
